title: teucrium flavum var. glaucum
intro: Teucrium flavum var. glaucum, also known as the shrubby germander, is a perennial plant species in the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its woody stems and silvery-green leaves. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Teucrium flavum var. glaucum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Teucrium flavum var. glaucum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
